calendar.matters: Petition By Prior Counsel For Approval Of Hourly Compensation For Extraordinary Attorney Services; Declaration Of Paul H. Melbostad, Esq.
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution

Petition By Prior Counsel For Approval Of Hourly Compensation For Extraordinary Attorney Services; Declaration Of Paul H. Melbostad, Esq.
Off Calendar Jun-03-2026 Continued To Aug-19-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jun-03-2026 Off Calendar

Petition By Prior Counsel For Approval Of Hourly Compensation For Extraordinary Attorney Services; Declaration Of Paul H. Melbostad, Esq.
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John F. Doyle for executor Rafael Rivera-Leon (via Zoom).
Paul Melbostad, prior counsel for executor (via Zoom).
Kimberly Stewart for objector Caren Ludmer (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Petition by Prior Counsel for Approval of Hourly Compensation for Extraordinary Fees; and 2) Status on Close of Estate.

The Court inquired as to the timing for filing a petition for final distribution. Mr. Doyle reported that the estate was prepared to file the petition, but that a recently received tax refund required inclusion in an inventory and appraisal and incorporation into the petition. He requested approximately one week.

The Court advised that any request for extraordinary fees will not be considered until after the petition for final distribution is filed, in compliance with California Rules of Court, rule 7.702, so that the issues may be addressed at the time of the hearing on the petition.

The matter was continued to June 3, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Off Calendar

Petition By Prior Counsel For Approval Of Hourly Compensation For Extraordinary Attorney Services; Declaration Of Paul H. Melbostad, Esq.
Off Calendar Feb-03-2026 Continued To Apr-07-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Doyle appeared via zoom for Rafael Rivera-Leon (petitioner).
Kimberly Stewart appeared via zoom for Caren Ludmer (objector).
Paul Melbostad appeared via zoom prior counsel for executor.

From the outset of the hearing Judge Russell S. Roeca disclosed that he represented Mr. Melbostad in the distance past.
The Court Address Mr. Melbostad's extraordinary fee request, That it's premature and the court is not inclined to grant any request prior to the final distribution, and that he is to supplement his request with statement of facts.

The report on status of administration is ordered off calendar.

Status on close of estate is set for April 07, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Petition for approval of hourly compensation for extraordinary fees is also continued to that date.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Obitinalla. Not reported.

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John F. Doyle for Rafael Divera-Leon, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Kimberly Stewart for Caren Ludmer, Respondent.
Howard Adams, trustee, is present (via Zoom).

Discussions are held regarding Ms. Ludmer's objections to the petition. Counsel for the administrator requested a one-year extension to resolve ongoing estate matters and noted ongoing discussions with opposing counsel to resolve these issues through mediation, which is expected to occur within the next two months.

Ms. Stewart expressed willingness to agree to a six-month timeline if progress is made concerning the minors involved in the estate, the lack of an inventory and appraisal, and the co-ownership of the house.

The court noted the lack of an inventory and appraisal, which is overdue.

The court took the matter under submission with intent to shorten the time requested to approximately six months, rather than the one year requested.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-22-2024 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated May 30, 2019 is admitted to probate. Charles Cheever is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for October 22, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, the status hearing will go off calendar. Frank Mahoney III is appointed Probate Referee.

Judge: Stephen Murphy: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Puliego: Not reported.
